# Daily Report — 2026-05-30

## Task Completed
Added `bulk_update_incidents` tool to `bulk_tools.py`. The tool accepts up to 100
`IncidentUpdate` entries (each carrying an `incident_id` plus any combination of
13 updatable fields), resolves incident numbers to sys_ids with a single batched
GET using the `numberIN` operator, then issues all PATCH calls in one Batch API
round-trip. Result entries are enriched with the original `incident_id` for easy
cross-referencing. Registered in `service_desk` and `full` packages (also added
`execute_bulk_operations` to `full`, which was previously missing).

## Test Results
- Total tests: 1308
- Coverage: 91%
- All passing: yes
- New tests: 23 (TestIsSysId, TestBulkUpdateIncidentsValidation,
  TestBulkUpdateIncidentsAllSysIds, TestBulkUpdateIncidentsNumberResolution)

## Security
Pre-existing dependency vulnerabilities only (setuptools, urllib3, pyjwt, wheel).
No issues introduced by this change.

## Files Changed
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/bulk_tools.py` — added `_is_sys_id`, `_INCIDENT_UPDATE_FIELDS`, `IncidentUpdate`, `BulkUpdateIncidentsParams`, `_resolve_incident_numbers`, `bulk_update_incidents`
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/__init__.py` — export `bulk_update_incidents`
- `src/servicenow_mcp/utils/tool_utils.py` — import and register `bulk_update_incidents`
- `config/tool_packages.yaml` — add to `service_desk` and `full` packages; add `execute_bulk_operations` to `full`
- `tests/test_bulk_update_incidents.py` — 23 new tests
